design_rationale: A goal‑directed control task requires dense guidance for learning efficiency. The primary progress signal is altitude descent (-y) because the task only succeeds near the ground. Horizontal centering is added via |x| penalty. To prevent crashing, velocity and tilt are penalised globally, with an extra penalty when close to the ground to force a soft touchdown. A sparse terminal success event gives a large reward for stable two‑leg contact within tight speed/angle bounds. A crash event penalises hard impacts. Fuel efficiency is encouraged by a control cost that penalises engine magnitudes. Together these elements shape a policy that descends carefully, lines up with the pad, and lands gently.
assumptions: The coordinate system has the landing pad center at x=0 and ground at y=0.
left_contact and right_contact become true when the corresponding leg touches the ground; both true signals a stable upright landing.
The episode terminates when the lander makes ground contact, so event_rules are evaluated exactly once at that step.
The allowed formula functions and primitive variables are exactly as listed; no additional derived quantities are available.
The default runtime action_penalty (‑sum(action^2)) is already applied, and our control_cost is an additional shaping component.
risk_notes: The constants (coefficients, thresholds) are not tuned to the specific environment; they may need empirical adjustment.
The y < 0.5 condition assumes a scale where 0.5 is a fraction of the starting altitude; if the environment uses a different unit, this will be ineffective.
Crash penalty might be unnecessary if dense shaping already strongly penalises high‑speed impacts, but it provides an explicit negative signal for safety.
canonicalization_notes: Event rule safe_landing: moved reward=100.0 into weight (old weight=1.0).
Event rule safe_landing: inferred semantic_role=terminal_success from one_time sign.
Event rule crash: moved reward=-50.0 into weight (old weight=1.0).
Event rule crash: inferred semantic_role=safety_constraint from one_time sign.

Component r_progress_guidance: normalized formula syntax from '-0.1 * y  - 0.05 * abs(x)  - 0.1 * abs(vy)  - 0.05 * abs(vx)  - 0.1 * abs(angle)  - 0.05 * abs(angular_velocity)' to '-0.1 * y - 0.05 * abs(x) - 0.1 * abs(vy) - 0.05 * abs(vx) - 0.1 * abs(angle) - 0.05 * abs(angular_velocity)'.
